messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] R64.app Won't Launch on 10.5.8 with a G5
messages.subject_clean: R64.app Won't Launch on 10.5.8 with a G5
messages.body_plain:
Hello all,

Is R64.app not backward compatible with 10.5 on a PPC64 machine? Does it only work if 10.5 is launched in 64 bit mode? 

Thanks,
Sean
messages.body_snippet:

Is R64.app not backward compatible with 10.5 on a PPC64 machine? Does it only work if 10.5 is launched in 64 bit mode?

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] R64.app Won't Launch on 10.5.8 with a G5
messages.subject_clean: R64.app Won't Launch on 10.5.8 with a G5
messages.body_plain:
On Apr 30, 2010, at 18:03, Sean Lake wrote:

> Hello all,
> 
> Is R64.app not backward compatible with 10.5 on a PPC64 machine? Does it only work if 10.5 is launched in 64 bit mode? 
> 
> Thanks,
> Sean

Sorry, forgot to add, it's R version 2.11.0 with GUI 1.33.

Thanks again,
Sean

> bounces at stat.math.ethz.ch] On Behalf Of Sean Lake

> Subject: Re: [R-SIG-Mac] R64.app Won't Launch on 10.5.8 with a G5
> 
> 
> On Apr 30, 2010, at 18:03, Sean Lake wrote:
> 
> > Hello all,
> >
> > Is R64.app not backward compatible with 10.5 on a PPC64 machine? Does
> it only work if 10.5 is launched in 64 bit mode?
> >
> > Thanks,
> > Sean
> 
> Sorry, forgot to add, it's R version 2.11.0 with GUI 1.33.
> 

>From the Mac OS X page on CRAN:
http://cran.r-project.org/
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Universal R 2.11.0 for with R.app GUI 1.33 released on 2010/04/27
This binary distribution of R and the GUI supports PowerPC (32-bit) and Intel (32-bit and 64-bit) based Macs on Mac OS X 10.5 (Leopard) and 10.6 (Snow Leopard).
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Only 32-bit is now built and supported for PPC.

OS X 10.5 is always 64-bit.  

The issue is that 64-bit PPC software/compilers and Apple
support are slowly disappearing as time goes on.  Simon Urbanek
and others that develop and maintain R for the Mac announced
a while back that PPC64 support would end, as the PPC platform
is becoming history.  So, it's either compile R and packages
from source in 64-bit mode yourself, or get a new Intel-based Mac.

HTH

Steve McKinney

> Thanks again,
> Sean
